Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We need craft answer Need deeply analyze POWL semantics models Need identify standard sequence edges direct precedence vs partial transitive Need anomalies

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Standard Hire to Retire perhaps post job ad then screen candidates then conduct interviews then make hiring decision then onboard employee then add payroll then close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model1 nodes all activities partial order edges Post to Screen Screen to Decide Screen to Interview Decide to Onboard Onboard to Payroll Payroll to Close No edge Interview to Decide Thus after screen Decide and Interview incomparable concurrent Decide can occur before or concurrent interview

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Onboard after Decide only not after Interview therefore onboarding can happen before interview Interview can even be later than close because Interview only constrained after Screen

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This anomaly is fundamental They likely intended Screen to Interview to Decide

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model1 maintains mandatory payroll and proper Decide to Onboard to Payroll to Close chain

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Model2 has loop onboarding and xor payroll with silent skip

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Loop Onboard skip means one or more onboarding occurrences Xor Payroll skip means payroll is optional

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Post to Screen and Post to Interview with Interview to Decide means Screen is unordered with Interview and Decide Therefore a decision can be made after interview but before screening Screen can even happen after close because it has no successors

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Repeated onboarding is a deviation unless it explicitly models retry correction Optional payroll after onboarding is serious because a hired onboarded employee should normally be added to payroll

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Both models can still produce the normative trace Post Screen Interview Decide Onboard Payroll Close so the anomalies are extra allowed behaviors rather than complete impossibility of the normal path

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model1 misses the dependency Interview before Decide while Model2 misses Screen before Interview and Screen before Decide and also allows repeated onboarding and skipping payroll

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Model1 is likely closer to the normative process because it has fewer and less numerous deviations It keeps all required activities mandatory exactly once and preserves the downstream administrative chain

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: If one treated repeated onboarding as intentional retry and optional payroll as a special business exception then Model2 could be viewed more favorably but the labels do not provide that justification

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final comparison favors Model1 because Model2 is more permissive and admits more invalid executions such as closing without payroll or screening after close
